lesson.title: MA & ARMA Models
lesson.body: A moving average model MA(q) is x_t = ΈΒ_t + ΈΗβ²¹ΈΒ_{t-1} + ... + ΈΗ_qΈΒ_{t-q}. Unlike AR, MA(q) is always stationary. It is invertible when roots of 1 + ΈΗβ²¹z + ... + ΈΗ_qzαΒ† lie outside the unit circle. The ARMA(p,q) combines both: x_t = ΈΘœÜαΒΔx_{t-i} + ΈΒ_t + ΈΘΈΗβ±ΦΈΒ_{t-j}. The ACF of MA(q) cuts off after lag q; the PACF tails off.

rooms.question: The ACF of an MA(q) process is:
answers: Nonzero for lags 1 to q, zero for lags > q (cuts off)
rooms.explanation: MA(q) ACF cuts off after lag q βÄî this is the key pattern to identify MA order. PACF tails off geometrically.

rooms.question: For MA(1): x_t = ΈΒ_t + ΈΗΈΒ_{t-1}, the variance is:
rooms.explanation: Since ΈΒ_t and ΈΒ_{t-1} are independent: Var(x_t) = œÉ¬≤_ΈΒ + ΈΗ¬≤œÉ¬≤_ΈΒ = œÉ¬≤_ΈΒ(1+ΈΗ¬≤).

rooms.question: Invertibility of an MA process is important because:
answers: It allows the process to be written as an AR(βàû) representation
It makes the ACF decay faster
rooms.explanation: An invertible MA can be inverted to an infinite AR: ΈΒ_t = x_t βà£ ΈΗβ²¹x_{t-1} βà£ ΈΗβ²¹¬≤x_{t-2} βà£ ... This enables forecasting from observed values.

rooms.question: For an ARMA(1,1): x_t = œÜx_{t-1} + ΈΒ_t + ΈΗΈΒ_{t-1}, the ACF:
answers: Decays geometrically at rate œÜ for lags βâΞ 2, with a different value at lag 1
rooms.explanation: The MA component affects only lag 1; for h βâΞ 2, œ¹(h) = œÜœ¹(h-1) βÄî geometric decay at rate œÜ. The ARMA signature is a mixed ACF.

rooms.question: Order the model identification steps using ACF and PACF patterns:
items: Plot sample ACF and PACF of the series
Check if ACF cuts off βÜ£ MA(q) candidate
Check if PACF cuts off βÜ£ AR(p) candidate
If both tail off, consider ARMA(p,q)
rooms.explanation: Box-Jenkins identification: plot, check ACF cutoff (MA), PACF cutoff (AR), both tail off (ARMA).

boss.name: The Moving Average
boss.taunt: I smooth your shocks. Each past error echoes in me, then fades.

questions.question: An MA(1) model ΈΗ and the MA(1) model with parameter 1/ΈΗ both generate:
answers: The same ACF (non-identifiability without invertibility constraint)
questions.explanation: ΈΗ and 1/ΈΗ produce the same ACF at lag 1: œ¹(1) = ΈΗ/(1+ΈΗ¬≤). Invertibility |ΈΗ| < 1 selects the unique parameterization.

questions.question: For MA(q), the one-step forecast xΧ²_{T+1|T} is:
answers: 0 (MA has no memory beyond q steps)
questions.explanation: The forecast uses estimated past innovations (residuals). Beyond q steps, all forecasts revert to zero (the process mean when ΈΦ=0).

questions.question: Which plot is most diagnostic for distinguishing AR(p) from MA(q)?
questions.explanation: ACF vs PACF is the classic Box-Jenkins identification tool: ACF cutoff βÜ£ MA, PACF cutoff βÜ£ AR, both tail off βÜ£ ARMA.
